rationale: The cited paper is used as a central theoretical anchor: the citing article repeatedly frames its argument against prior work on the idiosyncratic fit heuristic and explicitly states how its predictions differ from that prior focus. It also reuses and adapts the cited paper's empirical paradigm (e.g., The Economist fit setup, noted as based on Kivetz and Simonson 2003 Study 4) to test new hypotheses. In the discussion, the authors position their contribution as explaining and broadening mechanisms behind idiosyncratic fit effects rather than merely listing the paper as background.
relation_description: The citing paper directly builds on the idiosyncratic fit heuristic from Kivetz and Simonson (2003) and extends it by distinguishing unintended versus explicitly tailored fit while holding perceived fit constant. It adds new mechanism and boundary-condition claims (competitive outsmarting and seller intent) to that line of work.
